name: Snow Report 2026-03-25 16:00:00
report:
Good evening, Beast family, 

Clear and sunny skies met soft spring snow today and we had a blast shredding around. There’s more to be psyched about as this weekend looks to bring more sunshine. 

A little bit of everything rolls through overnight with spotty snow showers and a touch of mix, while winds ramp up and keep things moving. Thursday brings snow showers before 9:00 a.m., then scattered rain showers and strong winds. Temperatures will be pushing to 49 at the base and 43 at the top with just a trace of new snow at elevation.

Surface conditions are holding some hardpack still, but trails are softening up, especially where the sun shines strong like over at Bear. Skyeburst and Dream Maker had beautifully soft snow that made for a lot of fun. We could see some precipitation, but temperatures should stay well above freezing keeping surfaces on the softer trend. Head over to Bear and to Needles Eye for that more carvable snow. 

Our grooming team is adding Upper Wildfire to the list of trails that will be resurfaced by the time first chair rolls around. East Fall and Highline will be groomed and were holding decent surfaces. Head over to Caper or Bear Claw for a more chill ride that will also be smoothed over. For the full list of groomed options, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

Our snowmaking team took advantage of the cold stretch last week, firing on Snowshed Slope, Upper Skyelark, Lower Bittersweet, and Highlander. Stay tuned for what's to come as they’ll be getting the guns going again soon, and this is the latest they've ever had them running! If you haven’t yet, check out our latest Insider Blog to see how our recent snowmaking investments have been paying off. 

Our key lifts are once again scheduled to open again at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad following suit at 9:30 a.m. and our South Ridge Quad coming online at 10:00 a.m. 

In our kparks, the Bear parks (Dream Maker & The Stash) are back on the menu for some sweet spring skiing & riding. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Our Killington Parks Crew recently did a feature refresh in Timberline, and our halfpipe was re-cut again this past evening. Make sure you check out what the team has been cooking up! Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are also dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on all of our features. 

Last thing: if you can't get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199. 

Let’s get out there. 

- Evan

 

Operating Day: 135

Lifts            

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.           

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.            

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!            

The Tubing Park presented by bubly is closed for the season.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-25 05:50:02
report:
Good morning, Beast fans! 

We saw a big improvement in our snow surfaces yesterday after our groomers worked some more magic and today should bring more of the same sugary snow goodness on the slopes. We added a number of trails back to our count and are monitoring (and hoping for) some potential snow coming today/tomorrow. 

For today’s weather, we’re looking at temps starting in the high 20s and creeping up to the mid 30s by the time lifts stop spinning. Predominant cloud cover will return during the day, and stronger winds will open up the day before weakening a bit through the afternoon. 

Conditions out there recently have ranged from wintry in the morning to downright springy by the afternoon. My personal run of the day yesterday was Lower East Fall, which had some really fun snow that was soft but edge-able all the way down. I didn’t need to head to Bear Mountain to find the sun yesterday, but I made a quick trip over anyway. Lower Wildfire had very soft snow in the sun, while Middle Superstar held more of its bite in the shade. It’s going to be a while before that melts out! 

Our grooming covered much of our open terrain again, with Lower Double Dipper and Downdraft getting swapped out for other steeper pitches like Panic Button and Lower Pipe Dream. Get in those South Ridge laps while you can! Wildfire and Skyeburst will also provide paths for cruising down to the Bear this morning. For the full list of groomed options, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

Our key lifts are once again scheduled to open again at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad following suit at 9:30 a.m. and our Canyon and South Ridge Quads coming online at 10:00 a.m.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-24 05:43:05
report:
Good morning, Beast fans!

Today sees us reclaim about a dozen trails to our trail count after our groomers did their thing overnight to refresh most of our remaining surfaces. It was a bit of a tricky one out there yesterday, but conditions should continue to improve as this week progresses.  

The forecast today is calling for plenty of sun, plus relatively calm winds that should pick up slightly during the afternoon and temps in the 20s at our base. Not too dissimilar to yesterday overall (minus the sun), but steeper trails should be in a better spot out there. Some solid edges will still be your friend today!

Slow cruisers were the move yesterday, as conditions on our peak-to-creek route were soft and easy on the legs. (As a side note: get those laps down to Skyeship while you can! Our Skyeship Lodge will be closing after this Sunday.) Some scattered hardpack was out there on trails with more of a pitch, but our smoothest terrain had enough loose granular for you to grab onto. High Road has been a highlight of late, both as an escape route before Lower Superstar and as a fun trek into our Needles Eye area. 

Steeps will indeed be back on the menu today, as our groomers got the chance to tackle several more of them overnight. Double Dipper, Downdraft, and Cascade got a refresh before this morning, as well as other additions to the plan like Upper Pipe Dream, Needles Eye, and Wildfire. For the full list of groomed options, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

Our snowmaking team took advantage of the cold stretch this past week, with snowguns firing on Snowshed Slope, Upper Skyelark, Lower Bittersweet, and Highlander, along with fan guns around K-1, Snowdon, and Mouse Trap. And we're not calling it quits on snowmaking just yet—stay tuned for more updates there. 

Our key lifts are once again scheduled to open up at 9:00 a.m. this morning, with the North Ridge Quad following suit at 9:30 a.m. 

Great news for our park skiers & riders: our Bear parks (The Stash & Dream Maker) will reopen again today after a full night of grooming allowed us to get better conditions in both spots. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Our Killington Parks Crew recently did a feature refresh in Timberline, and our halfpipe was re-cut again this past evening. Make sure you check out what our team has been cooking up! Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are also dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on all of our features.

Just as a reminder, if you can't get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199. 

Every day on the slopes is a good day in my book. Enjoy it out there!

- Cullen

name: Snow Report 2026-03-22 16:00:35
report:
Good evening, Beast fans, 

We made it through another Sunday with just a few bouts of mixed precip falling from the sky, but temperatures softened things up out there and gave the surfaces a nice refresh from that recent firm stretch.

Expect a bit of everything overnight with rain, sleet, and some freezing rain in the mix. Tomorrow stays mostly cloudy with a few snow showers drifting through, but nothing major, as temps hover in the mid-20s with a steady north breeze.

The snow surfaces consisted of loose granular with scattered hardpack today, but things became more edge-able and fun as the day warmed up. Bunny Buster had some decent turns and trails in general made quite the improvement from yesterday. Tomorrow we are expecting surfaces to firm up once again so your sharper edges will be favorable for sure.  

Our grooming team will be after Bear Claw, Cruise Control, Upper Skylark, Lower Bittersweet, Bunny Buster, and many more tonight. Trails that receive a groom will be the go-to tomorrow with surfaces hardening as temps fall overnight. Head to our Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown of groomed options. 

Our snowmaking team took advantage of the cold stretch earlier this week, firing on Snowshed Slope, Upper Skyelark, Lower Bittersweet, and Highlander, along with fan guns around K-1, Snowdon, and Mouse Trap. Stay tuned for more that may be in the works—we’re eyeing a window next week where we could fire up once again. If you haven’t yet, check out our latest Insider Blog to see how our recent snowmaking investments have been paying off. 

Our key lifts are scheduled to open up at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad joining at 9:30 a.m. and the Canyon Quad at 10:00 a.m. 

Up in our kparks, Dream Maker is still firing with 16 features between the upper and lower sections, including our premier jump line and the sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain. The Stash is the place to be for a more natural feel, with plenty of creative stone and wooden features on the trail. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, while Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature.  

Lastly, if you can't get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199. 

Have a great start to you week! 

- Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-21 05:55:06
report:
Good morning, Beast fans,

It’s shaping up to be another fun weekend here at The Beast. We got the full range of wintry mix yesterday afternoon, with around 2” of snow coming in first before the less-wintry version of precipitation arrived during the last hour of the day. Conditions were largely fun and soft, and our groomers worked their magic overnight to get our surfaces ready again for the morning. 

The forecast is calling for temperatures to drop a bit leading into the morning. They will float right below freezing during most of the day, so we’re not anticipating any significant icing. Northwest winds will make it feel a little colder than 25° at the peak, but nothing unseasonably cold. 

Snow surfaces softened up yesterday before the wintry mix rolled in, making turns easy just about everywhere. Everyone was very kind and left me some corduroy to find on skier’s right of High Road this afternoon. I’d say either that or Lower Pipe Dream was my run of the day. Coverage is still nice, and the mix of small bumps and groomed surfaces there were great. 

Speaking of grooming, our team faithfully tackled 70+ trails last night with smoothed out steeps including Double Dipper and Cascade. To prep for our Mogul Camps this weekend, we’ll also have some bumps set up on Lower Bear Claw and Shorty to go along with the natural bumps on Low Road. Head to our Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown of groomed options. 

Our snowmaking team took advantage of the cold stretch earlier this week, firing on Snowshed Slope, Upper Skyelark, Lower Bittersweet, and Highlander, along with fan guns around K-1, Snowdon, and Mouse Trap. Stay tuned for more that may be in the works—we’re eyeing a window next week where we could fire up once again. If you haven’t yet, check out our latest Insider Blog to see how our recent snowmaking investments have been paying off. 

As a reminder, today is our last scheduled operating day for the Snowdon Triple and we'll be giving out complimentary donut holes at 9:30 a.m. today to celebrate.  A Killington staple since 1973, this historic lift will make way for a new, fixed-grip Doppelmayr quad chairlift this summer. As a special goodbye present, everyone who rides the lift today between 9:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. will be entered in to win one of the 194 chairs! We’ll be auctioning off the rest of the chairs later this spring—stay tuned for more info there. 

Along with the Snowdon Triple, we have 18 lifts scheduled to run, with the Bear Mountain Quad kicking things off at 7:30 a.m. Park over to Bear Mountain in the morning to grab the earliest laps we offer here at Killington. After that, our key lifts are scheduled to open up at 8:00 a.m., with the Canyon Quad and South Ridge Quad also joining the party at 9:00 a.m. 

Up in our kparks, Dream Maker is still firing with 16 features between the upper and lower sections, including our premier jump line and the sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain. The Stash is the place to be for a more natural feel, with plenty of creative stone and wooden features on the trail. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, while Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature.  

Lastly, if you can't get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199. 

Enjoy your weekend! 

- Evan
